name: The Everglades
notes: A maze of sawgrass, mangrove channels and gator holes. Largemouth bass and exotic peacock bass prowl the warm shallows; something with teeth always lurks.

species.name: Largemouth Bass
species.realInfo: Best on plastic worms and frogs worked through the pads at first light.

species.name: Peacock Bass
species.behavior: Aggressive exotic that hammers fast-moving lures.
species.realInfo: Introduced to canals in the '80s; loves warm, bright midday water.

species.name: Florida Gar
species.behavior: Armoured ambusher that rolls at the surface.

species.name: Bull Shark
species.behavior: Yes — they swim up the brackish channels. Hold on.
species.realInfo: Bull sharks tolerate freshwater and range far up Florida's rivers.

strategy: Throw weedless lures right into the lily pads — bass hide beneath them.
Peacock bass feed in bright midday sun, opposite to most species.
If the drag screams and won't stop… you didn't hook a bass.
